grid-plan.description: Analyse a bug or feature for a Servoy ag-grid component and write a plan. Usage: /grid-plan <component>: <description>. Components: powergrid | datagrid | datasettable | groupingtable
grid-plan.template:
You are invoking the servoy-grids analyst agent.

The user's request:
$ARGUMENTS

Run the `servoy-grids` agent with this exact request. The agent will:
1. Identify the component and intent (bug fix or new feature) from the request
2. Read the relevant source files deeply
3. Write a precise, self-contained implementation plan to `.opencode/plans/plan.md`
4. Display the full plan in the conversation

After the plan is displayed, tell the user: Run `/grid-implement` to implement the plan.

grid-implement.description: Implement the plan or fix review issues. Run after /grid or after /grid-review.
grid-implement.template:
You are invoking the servoy-grids-implementer agent.

Run the `servoy-grids-implementer` agent. The agent will:
1. Read `.opencode/plans/plan.md` in full
2. Check if `.opencode/plans/review.md` exists with a NEEDS CHANGES verdict
   - If yes: enter fix mode — apply only the critical issues from the review, then delete review.md
   - If no: fresh implementation — implement exactly what the plan specifies
3. For Angular components (powergrid, datagrid): run `npm run build` in `/home/gabi/github/aggridcomponents/aggrid/` and fix any compile errors
4. Report mode (fresh/fix), which files were changed, and the build result

After the implementation is complete, tell the user: Run `/grid-review` to review the implementation.

grid-revise.description: Revise the current plan. Usage: /grid-revise <feedback>. Without feedback, displays the current plan. Run after /grid.
grid-revise.template:
You are invoking the servoy-grids-reviser agent.

The user's revision feedback:
$ARGUMENTS

If $ARGUMENTS is empty or contains only whitespace, display the current plan verbatim and stop.
Otherwise, run the `servoy-grids-reviser` agent with this feedback. The agent will:
1. Read `.opencode/plans/plan.md`, apply the requested changes, rewrite the plan, and display it in full

After the plan is displayed, tell the user: Run `/grid-revise <feedback>` to alter further, or `/grid-implement` when ready to implement.

grid-review.description: Review the implementation against .opencode/plans/plan.md. Run after /grid-implement.
grid-review.template:
You are invoking the servoy-grids-reviewer agent.

Run the `servoy-grids-reviewer` agent. The agent will:
1. Read `.opencode/plans/plan.md`
2. Run `git diff` to see the actual implementation
3. Write a structured review to `.opencode/plans/review.md`
4. Output the full review to the conversation verbatim

The review verdict will be one of: APPROVED | NEEDS CHANGES | REJECTED.

If the verdict is NEEDS CHANGES, tell the user: Run `/grid-implement` to apply the fixes, then `/grid-review` again.

grid-restore.description: Restore an archived plan. Usage: /grid-restore [filename]. Without argument, lists available archives.
grid-restore.template:
You are handling a /grid-restore request.

The user's argument:
$ARGUMENTS

Workflow:

1. If $ARGUMENTS is empty or contains only whitespace:
   - List all `.md` files in `.opencode/plans/archive/` (excluding `-review.md` files)
   - Display them as a numbered list with their filenames
   - Ask the user which one to restore
   - Stop here and wait for the user's response

2. If $ARGUMENTS contains a filename or number:
   - Find the matching archive file in `.opencode/plans/archive/`
   - If the current `.opencode/plans/plan.md` exists, archive it first (same logic as /grid: read title, derive slug, move to archive with today's date)
   - Copy the selected archive file to `.opencode/plans/plan.md`
   - If a corresponding `-review.md` file exists in the archive (same prefix), copy it to `.opencode/plans/review.md`
   - Delete any existing `.opencode/plans/review.md` that does not belong to the restored plan
   - Display the restored plan content
   - Tell the user: Plan restored. Run `/grid-revise <feedback>` to alter it, or `/grid-implement` to implement it.
